mnemonics: Plan Quality Early (PQE): Prevents Quick Explosions. Think of planning for quality as a pre-emptive safety measure to stop your project from blowing up later.
keyPoints: Quality is a core project constraint, not an afterthought, and must be integrated into the initial project plan.
Planning for quality involves defining standards, metrics, and processes (like reviews and testing) from the start.
Proactive quality planning reduces long-term costs by preventing defects, unlike reactive approaches which lead to expensive fixes.
commonMistakes: Treating quality assurance (e.g., testing) as a final phase instead of an activity integrated throughout the entire project lifecycle.
Equating more features with higher quality, often at the expense of non-functional requirements like reliability, security, and maintainability.
